objective: Extend only the existing capability-gated one-channel one-record String runtime maximum from 127 to the LSLC-003Y-observed exact 128 UTF-8 bytes while preserving every prior value and boundary.
outputs: One independently authored exact-128-byte runtime case using existing LSLC-003Y framing evidence.
Typed 129-byte rejection plus preservation and cleanup coverage without any other production breadth.
Sanitized fixture, focused checker, and documentation/policy routes.
non_scope: Additional channels or records, values above 128 UTF-8 bytes, other length forms, non-loopback networking, ambient activation, descriptor/lock changes, devices, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, copied source, commands, or Manifold authority.

acceptance.proof: The capability-gated runtime preserves exactly one 128-byte String record with LSLC-003Y-observed framing and cleanup.
Existing zero-through-127 behavior, capability dependencies, activation closure, 129-byte rejection, damaged cases, and nonclaims remain exact.
Standard policy, public boundary, documentation, instructions, and workflow contracts pass.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Bound the exact 128-byte runtime addition and nonclaims.
Route the current String runtime envelope.
Document the capability and exact boundary.
Separate observed exact-128 compatibility from local implementation.
Route the focused runtime checker through policy.
Existing LSL lane and activation guidance applies.
Existing contract and authority guidance applies.

commit_policy: Commit implementation and lifecycle separately; preserve LSLC-003X zero-through-127 behavior and LSLC-003S activation exactly.
